name: Reduced-size_IDCT
operator_description: Performs reduced-size inverse DCT to produce smaller output blocks (2x2 or 4x4 pixels) directly from 8x8 DCT coefficient blocks. This enables efficient image scaling during JPEG decompression by extracting only low-frequency coefficients and applying simplified two-pass IDCT operations. The 2x2 variant produces a quarter-size output suitable for 4:1 scaling, while the 4x4 variant produces half-size output suitable for 2:1 scaling. Both implementations skip processing of high-frequency coefficients that would be discarded anyway, significantly improving decompression performance for scaled output.
